title: Good News for NCR: Dell Loses CIO to HP
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Kastner blog post on OnComputersTips commenting on Randy Mott leaving Dell for HP. Traces Mott's career: Walmart CIO (built NCR Teradata-driven supply chain), then Dell, then HP under CEO Mark Hurd (ex-NCR CEO). Kastner predicts HP will quietly become a major Teradata customer and argues there is no better technology for creating near-real-time transaction systems that drive operational excellence.
importance_rationale: Captures Kastner's Teradata thesis at a moment of major CIO-market movement; represents his conviction-level opinion on enterprise data-warehouse tech.
relevance_rationale: Teradata continues in service; HP-Teradata relationship did grow; CIO-following-CEO dynamic remains a common IT-procurement pattern.
prescience_rationale: HP did become a bigger Teradata customer in mid-2000s; Mott shook up HP IT. However, Mott was eventually ousted from HP in 2011 and HP itself split in 2015.
